description: Nine-run protected sequencing scan on the no-distance edge-graph control branch. The survivor update rule stays combinatorial, but each step is externally forced by the C0.3 incidence-noise menu, the C0.4 motif menu, or a hybrid chooser across both, while the protected topology-stability constraint remains active.
architecture_notice: This branch keeps graph size, adjacency and graph-shell kernels, the clustered / corridor / triad representative families, the projected-transverse sector, and the shell-weight measurement vocabulary fixed. Only the protected sequencing step is externally forced by incidence-noise or motif injections.
baseline_notice: C0.6 keeps the C0.5 protected topology-stability rule active, but deliberately injects the C0.3 and C0.4 combinatorial perturbation menus to test whether positive mismatch density and protected arrow forcing can be made reproducible.

run.notes: Protected sequencing with the exact C0.3 incidence-noise ladder on the clustered composite anchor.

forcing_protocol.label: Noise-seeded protected sequencing
forcing_protocol.definition: Use the exact C0.3 incidence-noise ladder as the protected graph forcing schedule.

run.notes: Protected sequencing with a hybrid chooser across the exact C0.3 and C0.4 forcing ladders on the clustered composite anchor.

forcing_protocol.label: Hybrid seeded protected sequencing
forcing_protocol.definition: At each step compare the scheduled noise and motif candidates and keep the protected candidate with the stronger mismatch forcing signal.
